content.problemStatement:
Given an array CCC of MMM integers and a square matrix AAA (with integer entries) of dimension N√óNN \times NN√óN, verify whether the following equation is true,

C0IN+C1A+C2A2+‚Ä¶CM‚ąí1AM‚ąí1‚Č°0N(mod998244353)C_0 I_{N} + C_1 A + C_2 A^2 + \dots C_{M-1} A^{M-1}  \equiv 0_N \pmod{998244353}C0‚ÄčIN‚Äč+C1‚ÄčA+C2‚ÄčA2+‚Ä¶CM‚ąí1‚ÄčAM‚ąí1‚Č°0N‚Äč(mod998244353)

where 0N0_N0N‚Äč is the square null matrix (matrix in which all elements are zero) and INI_NIN‚Äč is the identity matrix, both having dimensions N√óNN \times NN√óN.

**Note:**


Two matrices A,BA, BA,B each with integer entries are said to be congruent modulo MMM iff all entries of A‚ąíBA - BA‚ąíB are divisible by MMM. This is denoted as A‚Č°B(modM)A \equiv B \pmod{M}A‚Č°B(modM).
Since the input-output is large, prefer using fast input-output methods.

Input Format

The first line contains TTT denoting the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer MMM denoting the length of CCC.
The second line of each testcase contains MMM space separated integers, C0,C1,‚Ä¶CM‚ąí1C_0, C_1, \dots C_{M-1}C0‚Äč,C1‚Äč,‚Ä¶CM‚ąí1‚Äč representing the array CCC.
The third line of each testcase contains a single integer NNN denoting the size of the square matrix AAA.
The iii-th line of the next NNN lines contains NNN space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,NA_{i,1}, A_{i,2}, \dots, A_{i,N}Ai,1‚Äč,Ai,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai,N‚Äč denoting the elements of the iii-th row of the matrix AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line `YES` if the equation C0In+C1A+C2A2+‚Ä¶CM‚ąí1AM‚ąí1‚Č°0N(mod998244353)C_0 I_n + C_1 A + C_2 A^2 + \dots C_{M-1} A^{M-1}  \equiv 0_N \pmod{998244353}C0‚ÄčIn‚Äč+C1‚ÄčA+C2‚ÄčA2+‚Ä¶CM‚ąí1‚ÄčAM‚ąí1‚Č°0N‚Äč(mod998244353) satisfies, and `NO` otherwise.


Output is **case insensitive**, i.e., you may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Both test cases have the same co-efficients. Since 998244351‚Č°‚ąí2(mod998244353)998244351 \equiv -2 \pmod{998244353}998244351‚Č°‚ąí2(mod998244353) and 998244348‚Č°‚ąí5(mod998244353)998244348 \equiv -5 \pmod{998244353}998244348‚Č°‚ąí5(mod998244353), for convenience of explanation, we shall take the co-efficients as ‚ąí2,‚ąí5,1-2, -5, 1‚ąí2,‚ąí5,1. Note that this does not affect the answer.
Test case 111: The given matrix, A=[1234]A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix}A=[13‚Äč24‚Äč], satisfies the equation ‚ąí2I2‚ąí5A+A2=02-2 I_2 -5 A + A^2 = 0_2‚ąí2I2‚Äč‚ąí5A+A2=02‚Äč, so the answer is YES.
Test case 222: For the given matrix, A=[1111]A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{bmatrix}A=[11‚Äč11‚Äč], the left hand side of the equation evaluates to, ‚ąí2I2‚ąí5A+A2=[‚ąí5‚ąí3‚ąí3‚ąí5]-2I_2 - 5A + A^2 = \begin{bmatrix}-5 & -3\\-3 & -5\end{bmatrix}‚ąí2I2‚Äč‚ąí5A+A2=[‚ąí5‚ąí3‚Äč‚ąí3‚ąí5‚Äč]. Clearly none of the entries are divisible by 998244353998244353998244353, so the answer is NO.
